memo: STOP: Do not issue this notice. The draft approves cardiac rehabilitation correctly under MCR-CR but commits a QMB cost-sharing violation. The member is QMB-tier dual eligible and cannot be charged the $45 copay shown in the draft. Per DUAL-QMB: 'A member in the Qualified Medicare Beneficiary program cannot be charged Medicare deductibles, coinsurance, or copayments for covered services.' The file is complete, the clinical criteria are met (CABG on 2026-05-30 within the 12-month window; physician referral on file), and the decision itself is correct—but the member responsibility must be zeroed before any notice goes out. Correct the member_cost_share to $0.00, re-review for Medicaid coordination per DUAL-DSNP, and then route for final issuance.
member_impact: Your cardiac rehab program has been approved for 24 sessions. Because you are in the Qualified Medicare Beneficiary program, you do not owe any Medicare copays for this service. You will not receive a bill from Medicare for this program.
recommended_actions: Correct member_cost_share from $45.00 to $0.00 in the decision.
Verify Medicaid coordination: confirm state Medicaid has been notified and claim routing is set up per DUAL-DSNP.
Route to compliance review before final issuance to prevent a QMB violation notice.
Reissue the notice with zero cost sharing.

citations.quote: A member in the Qualified Medicare Beneficiary program cannot be charged Medicare deductibles, coinsurance, or copayments for covered services.
Any decision that imposes cost sharing on a QMB member is incorrect regardless of the coverage outcome.

Cardiac rehabilitation programs are covered for members who within the preceding twelve months have experienced a heart attack, coronary artery bypass surgery, stable angina, heart valve repair or replacement, coronary angioplasty or stenting, or a heart or heart-lung transplant.
A dual-eligible special needs plan must coordinate the member's Medicare and Medicaid benefits, including sharing information needed for the state to adjudicate its portion.

findings.detail: File is complete: all 2 required documents for cardiac_rehab are present.
Decision clock: 122 hours remain on the standard track (due 2026-07-15T11:00:00Z).
Member is QMB-tier dual eligible: zero Medicare cost sharing may be charged, and providers may not balance bill. Medicaid coordinates as secondary payer.
Draft decision imposes $45.00 cost sharing on a QMB member. QMB members owe $0 — this must be corrected before any notice goes out.

tool_input.query: QMB dual eligible cost sharing Medicare cost sharing

tool_trace.result_digest: query 'QMB dual eligible cost sharing Medicare cost sharing' → DUAL-QMB (20.946), DUAL-LAST (20.141), DUAL-DSNP (12.585), COV-PRECEDENCE (2.342)
query 'cardiac rehabilitation coverage criteria Medicare' → DOC-CR (8.472), MCR-CR (8.239), COV-PRECEDENCE (5.467), COV-INTERNAL (3.995)

escalate_reason: QMB cost-sharing violation: draft imposes $45 copay on a QMB member. Per DUAL-QMB, this is incorrect regardless of coverage outcome. File is complete and clinical criteria are met; compliance error alone requires routing for correction before notice issuance.
